showcase.title: Showcase website development
showcase.metaDescription: Premium showcase website development for independents, SMEs and brands. Based in Eure-et-Loir, I work locally and remotely with clear scoping and direct execution.
showcase.heroTitle: A showcase website that builds trust, clarifies your offer and drives inquiries
showcase.heroSubtitle: I design custom showcase websites for companies, firms and brands that need a clear, fast and credible online presence, locally or remotely.
showcase.problemTitle: An average business website rarely makes people want to reach out
problemItems: A generic design weakens your positioning and makes your offer feel interchangeable.
Slow or poorly structured pages cost both visibility and inquiries.
Unclear messaging leaves visitors without direction or confidence.
A difficult codebase turns every update into a small project.
showcase.solutionTitle: A showcase website designed as a useful business asset
solutionText: I shape the project around your offer, your proof points and your conversion path so every section has a clear role.
The result prioritizes readability, speed, search visibility and ease of management so the site becomes useful from day one.
You work directly with the developer who scopes, designs and delivers the project, without strategy and execution being split apart.
proofItems: Lean architecture, clean SEO setup and performance treated as a first-order concern.
Direct collaboration with one technical point of contact from scope to launch.
Smooth collaboration in Eure-et-Loir, in Paris or fully remote.
A modern base that can evolve without forcing a rebuild later.
showcase.techTitle: What I typically include in this kind of project
techItems: Presentation pages, service pages, contact flows and SEO-ready content structures.
Custom components, restrained motion and a smooth mobile experience.
CMS or simple content editing based on how autonomous you want to be after delivery.
Clean deployment, analytics setup and maintainable foundations.
showcase.processTitle: A simple method to move fast without losing quality

processSteps.title: Scoping
processSteps.description: We clarify your goals, target audience, content and launch constraints.

processSteps.title: Direction
processSteps.description: I define page structure, messaging, SEO priorities and the visual direction.

processSteps.title: Production
processSteps.description: The website is built with close attention to detail, performance and responsive quality.

processSteps.title: Launch
processSteps.description: I handle the final setup, checks and a clean handoff for what comes next.

showcase.whyChooseTitle: A clear and demanding working setup
whyChooseItems: Product direction and technical execution stay aligned within one collaboration.
Decisions are reasoned, priorities stay visible and delivery remains focused.
Clean launch process with post-launch follow-up when needed.
showcase.faqIntro: These are the points most often discussed before starting this kind of project.

faqMain.question: How long does it take to launch a showcase website?
faqMain.answer: With a well-scoped project, launch can happen within days or a few weeks depending on page count, content readiness and validation cycles.

faqMain.question: Can you write or refine the website copy?
faqMain.answer: Yes. I can restructure the messaging, condense what matters and help shape clearer, more persuasive content.

faqMain.question: Will the website be SEO-friendly?
faqMain.answer: Yes. Structure, performance, metadata and content are all designed to support search visibility from launch.

faqMain.question: Can the website evolve later?
faqMain.answer: Yes. The base is prepared for new pages, sections or forms without forcing a rebuild.

faqAdvanced.question: Do you only work with local clients?
faqAdvanced.answer: No. I can work locally in Eure-et-Loir when useful, but many projects run smoothly in a fully remote setup with a clear process.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you take over an existing website?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, after a quick review. Depending on the current state, I may recommend targeted improvements or a cleaner rebuild.

faqAdvanced.question: Will the website be easy to manage?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. When relevant, I can provide simple editing capabilities so you remain autonomous on useful content.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you also handle hosting?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. I can manage deployment, technical setup and propose a maintenance framework afterward.

faqAdvanced.question: How do feedback loops work during the project?
faqAdvanced.answer: Validation points are built into the process so decisions stay clear and end-of-project back-and-forth is reduced.

faqAdvanced.question: Do you offer post-launch support?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. Maintenance, support, optimization and further evolutions can all be planned depending on your needs.

showcase.finalCtaTitle: Need a cleaner, faster and more credible business website?
showcase.finalCtaText: I can help you frame the project clearly, set a realistic timeline and deliver a polished website that actually supports your business.

webapp.title: Web application & SaaS development
webapp.metaTitle: Web application and SaaS development | MK-Web
webapp.metaDescription: Custom web applications, internal tools and SaaS products with a product-first and maintainable approach. I work with local and remote teams alike.
webapp.heroTitle: Web applications and SaaS built around real workflows, not feature accumulation
webapp.heroSubtitle: I build internal tools, client platforms and SaaS products with clear product logic, strong foundations and progressive delivery, locally or remotely.
webapp.problemTitle: A useful web product is not just a list of features
problemItems: A poorly scoped MVP quickly becomes difficult to evolve.
A confusing interface slows adoption and increases support load.
Improvised integrations make the product fragile and expensive to maintain.
Lack of prioritization spreads the budget thin and delays value.
webapp.solutionTitle: I build a product base that is usable now and scalable later
solutionText: Each application is designed around the most critical workflows, with a clear line between what is essential, useful and secondary.
The goal is to deliver a clear, reliable and extensible version that serves real business use cases without unnecessary complexity.
You keep visibility on trade-offs, delivery rhythm and the actual state of the product throughout the project.
proofItems: Functional scoping, prioritization and technical choices aligned with real usage.
Maintainable architecture that can evolve without early technical debt.
Smooth collaboration with founders and teams, on site or remotely.
Useful incremental delivery instead of long opaque production tunnels.
webapp.techTitle: Typical scope on a web application
techItems: Authentication, roles, dashboards, client areas and internal back offices.
Business forms, automations, payments, notifications and API integrations.
Analytics, technical quality and a clean base for future iterations.
Architecture matched to product maturity and operational constraints.
webapp.processTitle: A process focused on results and clarity

processSteps.title: Scoping
processSteps.description: We turn business goals into user flows, screens and product priorities.

processSteps.title: Architecture
processSteps.description: I define the technical structure, data models and required integrations.

processSteps.title: Iterative delivery
processSteps.description: The product moves forward in short batches with regular validation and explicit trade-offs.

processSteps.title: Consolidation
processSteps.description: We stabilize, document and prepare what comes next based on observed usage.

webapp.whyChooseTitle: An approach suited to products still taking shape
whyChooseItems: Product thinking and technical execution stay aligned and focused.
Pragmatic scope splitting helps deliver value without waiting for a perfect version.
Documentation and decisions stay clear so you keep control of the project.
webapp.technologiesTitle: Technologies suited to modern web platforms
webapp.faqTitle: Questions about web applications and SaaS
webapp.faqIntro: A few useful answers to frame a custom web product properly.

faqMain.question: Do you work on MVPs or only on mature products?
faqMain.answer: Both. I can scope a useful first version or take over an existing product to make it more reliable, clearer and easier to evolve.

faqMain.question: Can you connect external services?
faqMain.answer: Yes. Payments, CRM, emailing, storage, analytics or internal tools can be integrated when they support a real use case.

faqMain.question: How do you define the right initial scope?
faqMain.answer: By starting from critical workflows, business constraints and product risk. The goal is to deliver what unlocks real value first.

faqMain.question: Can you take over an existing codebase?
faqMain.answer: Yes, after an audit. Depending on the current state, I may recommend consolidating the base or rebuilding some parts more cleanly.

faqAdvanced.question: How do you handle projects with remote teams?
faqAdvanced.answer: With clear scope splitting, regular validation points and lightweight but useful documentation. It works well even when the team is distributed.

faqAdvanced.question: What happens after the first delivery?
faqAdvanced.answer: I can stay involved for support, new iterations, maintenance or technical consolidation based on your rhythm.

faqAdvanced.question: Do you also work on product UX?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. I contribute to flow structure, screen hierarchy and interface clarity, not only implementation.

faqAdvanced.question: Can the product be SEO-friendly?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, when public pages need search visibility. In that case SEO is handled in the architecture and rendering strategy from the start.

faqAdvanced.question: How do you approach security?
faqAdvanced.answer: Data validation, access control, secret handling, updates and deployment hygiene are treated as part of the core foundation.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you deliver in batches?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. It is often the healthiest way to keep visibility and correct course quickly when needed.

webapp.finalCtaTitle: Need to structure a web product without drifting into avoidable complexity?
webapp.finalCtaText: I can help you scope a solid version, make the right trade-offs and deliver a web application or SaaS that is useful quickly.

mobileapp.title: Mobile app development
mobileapp.metaTitle: iOS and Android mobile app development | MK-Web
mobileapp.metaDescription: Custom mobile app development for iOS and Android with strong focus on usability, smooth performance and reliable delivery. I work with local and remote teams alike.
mobileapp.heroTitle: A mobile app that feels useful, fast and sustainable beyond the first launch
mobileapp.heroSubtitle: I design and build mobile applications for iOS and Android with a strong focus on user flows, product quality and long-term maintainability, locally or remotely.
mobileapp.problemTitle: A mobile app cannot afford approximation
problemItems: A confusing UX is penalized immediately by users.
Weak performance hurts usage, retention and product perception.
Unclear technical choices make maintenance and future releases harder.
App Store and Play Store delivery becomes painful without a clean process.
mobileapp.solutionTitle: I treat the app as a product, not just a mobile screen
solutionText: I first map the most important flows, platform constraints and friction points so the application stays clear in real usage.
Development follows a logic of reliability, readability and long-term maintainability, not just the need to ship a first version quickly.
The goal is to launch an app that is useful now and still viable six months or a year later.
proofItems: Work centered on real usage flows, not only on visual polish.
Explicit trade-offs between delivery speed, native quality and maintainability.
Support on publishing, product evolution and post-launch follow-up.
Collaboration compatible with internal product, design or engineering teams, locally or remotely.
mobileapp.techTitle: What a well-scoped mobile engagement typically covers
techItems: React Native apps or another approach based on actual product needs.
Auth, notifications, forms, synchronization, embedded flows and APIs.
Rendering quality, usage testing and close attention to performance on real devices.
Store preparation and support with iOS and Android publishing constraints.
mobileapp.processTitle: A mobile process centered on real usage

processSteps.title: Product framing
processSteps.description: We define target users, priority flows, scope and store-related constraints.

processSteps.title: Mobile UX
processSteps.description: I structure screens, transitions and critical actions so the app remains readable and fast.

processSteps.title: Development
processSteps.description: The app is built with strong attention to reliability, rendering quality and integrations.

processSteps.title: Publishing
processSteps.description: I prepare the required deliverables, technical setup and app store release process.

mobileapp.whyChooseTitle: An approach suited to apps that need to be truly used
whyChooseItems: Priority goes to actual user experience, not cosmetic demo effects.
Clean technical execution keeps future iterations from becoming fragile.
Direct working relationship that stays clear and remote-friendly.
mobileapp.faqIntro: The main points worth clarifying before starting a custom mobile product.

faqMain.question: Should we target iOS and Android from the start?
faqMain.answer: Not always. It depends on your users, budget and business priorities. The right answer comes from scoping, not from habit.

faqMain.question: Can you work on an existing mobile app?
faqMain.answer: Yes, after an audit. I can take over an existing base, fix critical issues or stabilize the structure before adding more features.

faqMain.question: Do you handle store publishing?
faqMain.answer: Yes. I can prepare the release flow, check prerequisites and support the publication process.

faqMain.question: How is post-launch follow-up handled?
faqMain.answer: As with web products, the healthiest setup is usually a clear maintenance and evolution framework so the app stays stable over time.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you handle push notifications?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, when they serve a real use case. They are integrated as part of product logic, not as decorative extras.

faqAdvanced.question: Can the app work with an existing API?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. I can rely on your current backend or recommend cleanup if the technical interface is too brittle.

faqAdvanced.question: How do you choose between native and cross-platform?
faqAdvanced.answer: Based on usage, budget, timeline and interaction requirements. The choice should serve the product, not a fixed preference.

faqAdvanced.question: Do you work remotely on this kind of project?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. Remote collaboration works very well for mobile projects when scope, validation and delivery batches are well structured.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you integrate analytics and tracking?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, to monitor adoption, retention and friction points without overloading the product.

faqAdvanced.question: What if the scope changes during the project?
faqAdvanced.answer: The process is built for that. We re-prioritize batches and keep visibility on the impact in time and budget.

mobileapp.finalCtaTitle: Need a serious mobile app rather than a fragile prototype?
mobileapp.finalCtaText: I can help you frame the right scope, choose the right approach and launch a mobile app that is useful, reliable and ready to evolve.

ecommerce.title: E-commerce development
ecommerce.metaDescription: Fast, reliable and structured e-commerce websites built to sell better. I work in Eure-et-Loir and remotely on stores designed for conversion and day-to-day operations.
ecommerce.heroTitle: An e-commerce website built to sell more cleanly and run with less friction
ecommerce.heroSubtitle: I build custom e-commerce websites for brands and merchants who need a credible, fast and easier-to-evolve storefront, locally or remotely.
ecommerce.problemTitle: An online store quickly loses revenue when the fundamentals are weak
problemItems: A confusing checkout flow increases cart abandonment.
Weak mobile performance damages conversion.
Poorly structured product pages make SEO, trust and buying harder.
A messy back office or bad integrations make daily operations painful.
ecommerce.solutionTitle: I build stores that are clearer, faster and easier to run
solutionText: The work covers both buying experience and the technical structure and integrations that support the business every day.
The goal is to simplify the path to purchase, reinforce trust and build a base that can support your catalog, traffic and operations over time.
The project stays focused on what improves sales and saves time, not on feature accumulation.
proofItems: Purchase journeys clarified to reduce friction at the decisive moment.
Performance treated as a priority, especially on mobile and conversion pages.
Integrations selected to support the store, not to complicate it.
Execution compatible with local collaboration or a fully remote setup.
ecommerce.techTitle: What a well-structured e-commerce project covers
techItems: Catalog, product pages, category pages, checkout and useful content sections.
Payments, transactional emails, analytics, automations and business integrations.
SEO and performance work to support acquisition and conversion.
A clean base that can evolve without breaking things at every new request.
ecommerce.processTitle: A process centered on selling and operating cleanly

processSteps.title: Audit & scoping
processSteps.description: We clarify catalog structure, business priorities, technical constraints and conversion goals.

processSteps.title: E-commerce structure
processSteps.description: I work on navigation, product pages, reassurance and checkout with clarity as the main principle.

processSteps.title: Development
processSteps.description: The store is built with the right integrations and reliable foundations.

processSteps.title: Optimization
processSteps.description: I finalize performance, setup, quality checks and launch guidance.

ecommerce.whyChooseTitle: An e-commerce approach that is pragmatic rather than decorative
whyChooseItems: The project stays focused on conversion, speed and operational clarity.
Technical trade-offs serve the store and your real organization.
You keep one direct and committed point of contact.
ecommerce.faqIntro: The key topics worth clarifying before launching or rebuilding an online store.

faqMain.question: What budget should be expected for a custom store?
faqMain.answer: It depends on the catalog, checkout complexity, integrations and level of customization. The first step is always to scope priorities properly.

faqMain.question: Can you take over an existing store?
faqMain.answer: Yes. I can audit the current setup and recommend either focused improvements or a deeper redesign depending on technical debt and business goals.

faqMain.question: Do you also work on e-commerce SEO?
faqMain.answer: Yes. Category structure, product pages, performance and content foundations are all part of the work.

faqMain.question: Will the back office stay simple?
faqMain.answer: That matters a lot. A store should not only convert well for users but also remain easy to operate for the team behind it.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you connect payments, CRM or email tools?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, when those integrations support a concrete use case and fit cleanly into your operations.

faqAdvanced.question: Will the store be mobile-friendly?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. It is treated as a priority because a major part of browsing and buying happens on mobile.

faqAdvanced.question: How do you reduce cart abandonment?
faqAdvanced.answer: By clarifying the checkout, strengthening reassurance and removing unnecessary friction.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you work with a remote team?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. Most e-commerce projects move very well in a remote setup with a clean validation process.

faqAdvanced.question: Do you provide post-launch follow-up?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. Maintenance, optimization and further iterations can be planned after launch.

faqAdvanced.question: Can the store evolve later?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. The base is designed to support new categories, content, integrations or flows over time.

ecommerce.finalCtaTitle: Need a store that is stronger, faster and easier to operate?
ecommerce.finalCtaText: I can help you scope a custom e-commerce website that supports both your sales and your day-to-day operations more effectively.

redesign.title: Website redesign & audit
redesign.metaDescription: Technical, UX and SEO audit followed by a cleaner website redesign. I work in Eure-et-Loir and remotely to bring websites back in line with real business goals.
redesign.heroTitle: A useful redesign starts by understanding what is actually blocking results
redesign.heroSubtitle: I analyze the current website, isolate the real SEO, UX and technical friction points, then propose a redesign proportionate to your goals, locally or remotely.
redesign.problemTitle: Redesigning without diagnosis usually leads to bad trade-offs
problemItems: A website can age badly without the root cause being obvious.
Visible symptoms often hide deeper structural or technical debt issues.
A visual redesign alone may leave SEO and conversion problems untouched.
Poorly prepared migrations can break more than they improve.
redesign.solutionTitle: I start with a useful audit before deciding what should change
solutionText: The work begins with a structured diagnosis of performance, SEO, UX, content and technical foundations so the essential is separated from the secondary.
I then recommend the right level of redesign: focused improvements, partial rebuild or broader reconstruction when that is clearly the healthier path.
The goal is to rebuild visibility, clarity and maintainability without pushing the wrong investment.
proofItems: Audits are readable, prioritized and tied to concrete traffic, conversion and maintenance impact.
Redesign decisions are guided by facts, not only by the desire to look more modern.
Migration is handled carefully to avoid avoidable SEO losses and regressions.
The whole process works well in a remote collaboration model too.
redesign.techTitle: What a serious redesign actually covers
techItems: Performance audit, technical SEO review, page structure and journey clarity.
Redesign of components, content and calls to action when needed.
Technical reset when the existing base is blocking too many future changes.
Migration plan and verification before production launch.
redesign.processTitle: A useful redesign follows a simple order

processSteps.title: Audit
processSteps.description: I analyze the current setup, its limits and their real impact on your business.

processSteps.title: Prioritization
processSteps.description: I rank what should be fixed first based on impact and effort.

processSteps.title: Redesign
processSteps.description: I rebuild the most important parts on a clearer, more modern foundation.

processSteps.title: Launch
processSteps.description: I secure migration, quality checks and the move to production.

redesign.whyChooseTitle: A redesign approach that is more lucid than cosmetic
whyChooseItems: Diagnosis prevents decisions based only on intuition or visual taste.
Prioritization helps invest first where the gains will actually matter.
The project stays understandable, structured and compatible with phased delivery.
redesign.faqTitle: Questions about website redesign and audit
redesign.faqIntro: The topics that matter most when a website no longer delivers the expected results.

faqMain.question: When should a redesign be considered?
faqMain.answer: When the website no longer matches your positioning, blocks evolution or stops supporting visibility and conversion properly.

faqMain.question: Can an audit be enough?
faqMain.answer: Yes. In some cases, focused corrections create more value than a full redesign.

faqMain.question: Can a redesign hurt SEO?
faqMain.answer: Yes if it is handled poorly. That is why structure, redirects and migration checks matter.

faqMain.question: Can you audit a website built by another agency?
faqMain.answer: Yes. The analysis is based on technical and business facts, not on who built the site.

faqAdvanced.question: How do you choose between improvement and rebuild?
faqAdvanced.answer: I compare the cost of correction, the level of technical debt and the realistic improvement potential before recommending the healthiest path.

faqAdvanced.question: Is the design always rebuilt?
faqAdvanced.answer: Not necessarily. Sometimes the biggest gains come from structure, content or technical foundations instead.

faqAdvanced.question: Can local SEO be improved during the redesign?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. Redesign is often the right moment to rework structure, content and geographic relevance signals.

faqAdvanced.question: How does the handoff work?
faqAdvanced.answer: With a clear summary of decisions, completed checks and the points that should still be monitored after launch.

faqAdvanced.question: Can the redesign happen in phases?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. When it makes more sense, the work can be split to reduce risk and spread the investment.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you manage the project remotely?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. Audit, trade-offs, validation and follow-up all work very well remotely with a useful level of documentation.

redesign.finalCtaTitle: Does your website feel like more of a blocker than a lever?
redesign.finalCtaText: I can help you identify what is actually wrong and choose a redesign path that is healthier, clearer and more profitable.

maintenance.title: Website maintenance & support
maintenance.metaDescription: Website maintenance, technical support and focused evolutions for stable and secure platforms. I work in Eure-et-Loir and remotely to keep websites reliable over time.
maintenance.heroTitle: A reliable website is maintained over time, not only built once
maintenance.heroSubtitle: I handle maintenance, support and targeted evolutions so a website does not become slow, fragile or blocking, whether you are in Eure-et-Loir or working with me remotely.
maintenance.problemTitle: Without follow-up, a website quickly accumulates small issues and real emergencies
problemItems: Updates get postponed until a bug or security issue appears.
Performance gradually degrades without real monitoring.
Small changes become difficult to scope and resolve quickly.
Every issue turns into an emergency because no support framework exists.
maintenance.solutionTitle: I provide technical follow-up that is simple, clear and useful
solutionText: Maintenance covers what keeps your website healthy over time: updates, monitoring, fixes, targeted improvements and support on blocking points.
The aim is not to accumulate support hours but to keep a clean foundation that reduces emergencies and makes useful interventions faster.
You keep a direct point of contact who already knows the platform and does not need to rediscover it from scratch each time.
proofItems: Support is more direct and more readable than ad hoc interventions.
Work benefits from real familiarity with the underlying codebase.
Maintenance can cover a showcase website, business platform or e-commerce store.
The model works for both local and remote collaboration.
techItems: Updates, monitoring, fixes and stabilization of sensitive areas.
Targeted improvements on performance, UX and technical foundations.
Takeover of existing projects after a proper reading and stabilization phase.
Preparation of future evolutions on a cleaner base.
maintenance.processTitle: Ongoing follow-up is more useful than recurring emergencies

processSteps.title: Takeover
processSteps.description: I review the codebase, hosting, risks and recurring needs.

processSteps.title: Stabilization
processSteps.description: I first address urgent or overly fragile parts to reset the baseline.

processSteps.title: Follow-up
processSteps.description: I manage updates, monitoring and reasonable change requests.

processSteps.title: Improvement
processSteps.description: I recommend useful optimizations as your priorities shift.

maintenance.whyChooseTitle: Support that is more direct, contextual and sustainable
whyChooseItems: You do not need to re-explain the site history every time something happens.
Interventions happen within a readable framework rather than constant reaction mode.
You keep strong technical coverage without internalizing the whole burden.
maintenance.faqTitle: Questions about maintenance and technical support
maintenance.faqIntro: The main points worth clarifying before handing over the technical follow-up of a website.

faqMain.question: Can you maintain a website you did not build?
faqMain.answer: Yes, after a takeover phase. The goal is to understand the base before entering a real support rhythm.

faqMain.question: Does maintenance include improvements?
faqMain.answer: Yes, within reason. Larger changes are then handled through dedicated scoping.

faqMain.question: Can maintenance help SEO too?
faqMain.answer: Indirectly yes, because a fast, stable and technically clean site supports visibility better over time.

faqMain.question: How are urgent bugs handled?
faqMain.answer: A support framework helps resolve urgent issues faster because the base is already known and monitored.

faqAdvanced.question: Do you handle backups and monitoring?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, depending on the environment and project needs, to reduce risk and shorten reaction time when something goes wrong.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you take over a very unstable website?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, but an initial stabilization phase is often needed before regular maintenance becomes effective.

faqAdvanced.question: Does support work remotely too?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes. It is in fact the most common setup, with simple follow-up and prioritized requests.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you maintain an e-commerce store as well as a showcase website?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, although the points of attention differ. E-commerce often requires closer attention to integrations and operational continuity.

faqAdvanced.question: How do I know whether I need an audit first?
faqAdvanced.answer: If the base is unclear, slow or full of uncertainty, a quick audit often helps reset things more cleanly.

faqAdvanced.question: Can you stay involved over time?
faqAdvanced.answer: Yes, depending on the framework we define. The goal is to keep follow-up aligned with your rhythm and priorities.

maintenance.finalCtaTitle: Looking for technical support that is more reliable and less emergency-driven?
maintenance.finalCtaText: I can help you reset the technical baseline and maintain the platform inside a simple, stable and sustainable framework.
